frameworks.name: transcranial direct current stimulation (tDCS)
frameworks.one_line: non-invasive neuromodulation whose induced fields and BOLD response are imaged concurrently

frameworks.name: dual-echo echo-planar imaging (DE-EPI)
frameworks.one_line: phase encodes tDCS magnetic field while magnitude encodes BOLD contrast

frameworks.name: BOLD resting-state functional MRI
frameworks.one_line: used to read out brain's network-level response to applied current

core_question: Can MRI concurrently map tDCS-induced electromagnetic fields and the brain's BOLD response during stimulation?
key_assumptions: tDCS current produces a detectable magnetic-field perturbation in MRI phase
magnitude and phase of DE-EPI can be dissociated into BOLD and field maps
test-retest reliability is sufficient to detect group-level effects
manipulations: applied tDCS current under cathodal vs anodal electrodes
current on vs off during DE-EPI scanning
key_findings: tDCS-induced magnetic fields reliably detected in-vivo across test-retest
concurrent BOLD revealed canonical resting-state networks plus cathodal and anodal components under each electrode
cathodal BOLD signal significantly decreased with applied current at group level
interpretations: DE-EPI provides a joint readout of tDCS current markers and neurophysiological response
concurrent imaging can help explain inter-subject variability that hampers tDCS clinical translation
